title: Cynodon monostachyus
intro: Cynodon dactylon, also known as Cynodon monostachyus, is a perennial grass commonly referred to as Bermuda grass or Dhoob. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cynodon dactylon include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Cynodon dactylon is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Cynodon monostachyus is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Cynodon, which is a group of grasses commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The family Poaceae is a large and diverse group of plants, commonly known as true grasses. Cynodon monostachyus is a type of grass that is widely distributed across various parts of the world. Its classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf structure and seed head formation.

preparations: Cynodon monostachyus, commonly known as the burr grass, has limited information available regarding its traditional or herbal preparations.
